title: Unsafe shell command execution in Qubes wrapper
description: Usage of `os.system` invokes a shell and can be dangerous if arguments are dynamic. Even with static arguments, `subprocess` is preferred for better handling.
rationale: Easy to replace `os.system('reboot')` with `subprocess.run(['reboot'], check=True)` which is safer and standard practice.

title: Unsafe command line parsing in CPU device plugin
description: The function `g_spawn_command_line_sync` parses the command line string, which can lead to incorrect execution or injection if the path contains spaces or special characters.
rationale: Using `g_spawn_sync` with an explicit argument vector avoids shell parsing issues and is robust against paths with spaces.

title: Unsafe command line parsing for macOS version check
description: The function `g_spawn_command_line_sync` is used to execute `sw_vers`, which avoids proper argument escaping and path handling found in `g_spawn_sync`.
rationale: Replacing with `g_spawn_sync` and an argument array ensures safe execution regardless of the path returned by `g_find_program_in_path`.
